seo.title: Whisper vs Deepgram vs AssemblyAI
seo.description: A practical comparison of Whisper, Deepgram, and AssemblyAI for Voice & Speech AI.

content.intro: Selecting a voice AI provider requires balancing transcription latency against the depth of post-processing intelligence and total cost of ownership. This comparison evaluates the leading APIs for speech-to-text (STT) and voice intelligence based on implementation effort and performance in production environments.

options.name: Deepgram
options.tagline: Specialized high-speed inference engine for real-time streaming applications.
options.best_for: Low-latency conversational AI and high-volume real-time transcription.

options.name: AssemblyAI
options.tagline: Feature-rich API focused on developer experience and integrated LLM analysis.
options.best_for: Batch processing with complex requirements like summarization and sentiment analysis.

options.name: OpenAI Whisper
options.tagline: General-purpose robust transcription model with flexible deployment options.
options.best_for: Applications requiring high accuracy across diverse accents or self-hosted privacy.

criteria.name: Real-time Latency
criteria.description: The time elapsed between audio transmission and receipt of transcription results.

values.Deepgram: Sub-300ms latency via WebSocket streaming; optimized for live interactions.
values.AssemblyAI: Supports streaming but typically higher latency than Deepgram; optimized for accuracy over speed.
values.OpenAI Whisper: Standard Whisper is batch-oriented; OpenAI Realtime API offers low latency but at significantly higher cost.

criteria.name: Cost Profile
criteria.description: Standard pricing per minute of audio processed.

values.Deepgram: Approximately $0.0043/min for Nova-2 model; high volume discounts available.
values.AssemblyAI: Approximately $0.015/min for core transcription; additional costs for 'Audio Intelligence' features.
values.OpenAI Whisper: $0.006/min for hosted API; free (compute only) for self-hosted open-source version.

criteria.name: Deployment Flexibility
criteria.description: Options for hosting and data residency compliance.

values.Deepgram: Cloud-hosted and on-premise/private cloud deployment options available.
values.AssemblyAI: Cloud-only API service; no self-hosting option.
values.OpenAI Whisper: Fully open-source model weights allow for local, air-gapped, or custom cloud hosting.

criteria.name: Language Support
criteria.description: Breadth of supported languages and automatic language detection capabilities.

values.Deepgram: 30+ languages with specific models for specialized domains like medical or finance.
values.AssemblyAI: 80+ languages with robust automatic language detection and switching.
values.OpenAI Whisper: 99 languages; exceptional performance on low-resource languages due to massive training set.

criteria.name: PII Redaction
criteria.description: Built-in capability to identify and mask sensitive personal information.

values.Deepgram: Supports redaction of SSNs, credit card numbers, and emails via API parameter.
values.AssemblyAI: Sophisticated PII redaction including entity identification (names, locations) with high precision.
values.OpenAI Whisper: No native redaction in the model; requires post-processing with secondary LLMs.

criteria.name: Speaker Diarization
criteria.description: The ability to distinguish between different speakers in a single audio stream.

values.Deepgram: Fast, real-time diarization; sometimes struggles with overlapping speech.
values.AssemblyAI: High-accuracy diarization with speaker count detection and turn-taking logic.
values.OpenAI Whisper: Not natively supported in the base model; requires third-party tools like Pyannote.

criteria.name: Audio Intelligence Features
criteria.description: Native capabilities for summarization, sentiment, and intent detection.

values.Deepgram: Offers basic summarization and topic detection as add-on features.
values.AssemblyAI: Extensive 'LeMUR' framework allows running LLM prompts directly against transcripts.
values.OpenAI Whisper: Requires piping transcript output into GPT-4o for intelligence tasks.

criteria.name: Implementation Effort
criteria.description: Complexity of SDKs, documentation, and initial authentication setup.

values.Deepgram: Moderate; requires managing WebSocket states for real-time features.
values.AssemblyAI: Low; very polished SDKs and clear documentation for REST and WebSocket interfaces.
values.OpenAI Whisper: Variable; API is simple, but self-hosting requires significant DevOps and GPU management.

criteria.name: Resilience to Noise
criteria.description: Accuracy levels in environments with background noise or poor microphone quality.

values.Deepgram: Highly resilient via specialized 'Nova' models trained on diverse real-world audio.
values.AssemblyAI: Strong performance in telephonic and meeting environments.
values.OpenAI Whisper: Industry-leading robustness to background noise and technical artifacts.

verdict.summary: Deepgram is the technical choice for real-time, low-latency infrastructure where speed and cost-efficiency are paramount. AssemblyAI provides the best developer experience for post-call analysis and complex data extraction. OpenAI Whisper is the standard for high-accuracy batch processing and applications where data privacy necessitates self-hosting.

use_cases.scenario: Building a live AI voice assistant for a mobile app.
use_cases.reason: The sub-300ms latency is critical for natural turn-taking in conversational AI.

use_cases.scenario: Automated call center auditing and compliance reporting.
use_cases.reason: Native PII redaction and LeMUR intelligence simplify the extraction of structured data from calls.

use_cases.scenario: On-premise transcription for a healthcare provider.
use_cases.reason: Allows for deployment on local hardware to ensure HIPAA compliance without data leaving the network.
